concerns.text: FR-002 mandates generating '200 diverse, multi-turn task scenarios' but provides no verifiable definition of 'diverse' or the specific rules for the 'rule-based Python script'. Without a defined taxonomy or generation algorithm, this requirement is not testable (deterministic or reproducible).

FR-005 requires 'simple entailment checks' for Hallucination Rate but does not specify the algorithm (e.g., specific NLI model, regex patterns, or logic rules). 'Simple' is subjective; the requirement must name the specific deterministic procedure to be verifiable.

FR-008 requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping thresholds over '{0.01, 0.05, 0.1}' but does not specify which metric's threshold is being swept (Heuristic Adherence, Style Consistency, or Hallucination Rate). The test procedure is ambiguous.

SC-002 sets a 'non-inferiority margin of ±5%' for Style Consistency but does not define the baseline unit (absolute percentage points or relative % change). '±5%' is ambiguous without a reference unit (e.g., '5 percentage points' vs '5% of the baseline value').

SC-003 sets a '6-hour limit' for total compute time but does not specify the hardware configuration (CPU model, clock speed, core count) against which this is measured. Time is hardware-dependent; the success criterion is not measurable without a fixed reference environment.
